rationale: The cited paper is used as a concrete analytical guide for probing interaction effects, not just as background. In multiple studies, the authors explicitly report follow-up spotlight and floodlight (Johnson-Neyman) analyses and cite Spiller et al. (2013) for that procedure. This citation directly supports how key moderation results are tested and interpreted in the empirical analyses.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s moderated-regression probing framework to interpret interactions in its models. It uses spotlight and Johnson-Neyman floodlight analyses to determine conditional effects of joint goal progress across levels of relationship power.
